title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Cap… · Docker Systems Brief: Docker Capstone Production Defense
summary: An operations board must approve a production container system through daemon upgrade, disk pressure, backup recovery, credential rotation, and migration pressure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker capstone production defense increment in production Docker operations and release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.
State when Compose is sufficient and when scheduling, replicas, rolling updates, secrets, policy, load balancing, self-healing, or multi-host needs another platform.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Docker Capstone Production Defense contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release. A defensible solution applies Docker Capstone Production Defense, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production Docker operations and release defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-capstone-production-defense — Defend architecture, image identity, build, runtime, storage, network, Compose, security, tests, observability, supply chain, recovery, accessibility, and rollback as one release.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-4
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-4
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: worked docker production operations defense 4
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing isolated technical checks removes the need for an end-to-end production defense. This fails the Docker Capstone Production Defense contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
